dry_bulk.confluence_note: Spot rates are at the 99th percentile across major classes despite a multi-month streak of negative fundamentals reported by Breakwave.
dry_bulk.summary: The dry bulk market is in a technical expansion phase with the BDI at 2675 (Z=+2.24), yet this strength contrasts with weak Chinese steel production (-3.6% YoY) and record iron ore inventories (166mt). Market momentum remains positive even as long-term demand signals from the Far East remain subdued.
dry_bulk.outlook: The market is likely overextended in the short term, requiring a fundamental shift in Chinese steel demand to sustain these levels.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese iron ore portside inventory levels and steel mill margins.

tanker.confluence_note: Extremely high quantitative Z-scores align with positive fundamental assessments driven by geopolitical supply disruptions.
tanker.summary: Tanker markets are experiencing a historic rally, with Clean Tankers posting a Z-score of +3.21 and a staggering 147% ROC60. Geopolitical instability and the closure of key straits have throttled supply, turning a 'worst-case scenario' into a sustained period of high spot rates. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2198.0 (ROC60 +147.0%), alongside dirty tankers at 2854.0 (ROC60 +75.0%).
key_signals: Global Crude Oil On Water -0.4% YoY
VLCC Middle East-Asia rates up 71.1% YoY
tanker.outlook: Ongoing Middle East conflict and inventory draws will keep tanker rates elevated and volatile.
tanker.watch: Straits navigability status and OECD crude oil stock levels.

macro_note: Global trade is grappling with a sharp divergence: dry bulk is buoyed by technical momentum despite industrial headwinds, while the energy complex is in a supply-driven bull cycle fueled by escalating geopolitical risk.
